BUS_ASC_NAME: Black Elk Energy Offshore Operations, LLC
OPERATIONAL_NARRATIVE: Set DX plug

This operation was designed to set thru tubing plugs in the lower nipple profile and tubing hanger in preparation for repair fo the enomalous casign pressure location. Due to scale build up in the well, this objective has been postponed until a intervention can be designed to remove the scale build up and allow access to the lower well bore. Diagnostic logs to investigate the wellbore for sources of the pressure anomoliy where run in the upper accessable portions of the wellbore but where found to be inconclusive. Insitu Fluid samples where taken from the well for purposes of measuring asphaltene onset properties of the oil. Xylene was bullheaded into the well to reduce the operations risk of running wire in the wellbore.

The submitted wellbore diagram for this EOR to PA has a 2-7/8" CIBP set at 11,040' with no cement on top as per WAR report date February 24, 2008. The wellbore diagram on record for the EOR to TA  shows a 7-5/8" CIBP set at 12,140' with 33' cmt bailed on top, TOC 12,107'.

Bois D'Arc , Operator of record in 2008, TA'd this well and submitted an EOR March 2008. The subject EOR was approved and accepted inclusive of all associated operations and testing for the subject well. Stone APM to PA well entailed only cutting the respective casing strings below the mud-line. The submitted EOR is reflective of Stone activity to cut all casing strings below the mud-line utilizing the approved EOR TA diagram and associated work of the previous operator. 

The cementing of well conductors and the pressure testing of casing annuli were not part of the well work procedures set forth in the approved APM to PA dated June 7, 2010 and, as such, were not performed as part of the well PA  operations.  The requested information should have been provided by the Operator of record when the well was Temporarily Abandoned (TA) in 2008 and should be contained in BSEE records.
